frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: combines subject fMRI observation with learned atlas prior to infer retinotopic maps

frameworks.name: retinotopic atlas
frameworks.one_line: learned cortical atlas serves as the prior in the Bayesian mapping model

frameworks.name: voxel-wise fMRI mapping
frameworks.one_line: standard per-voxel analysis is limited by noise and subjective boundary calls

core_question: Can combining a learned retinotopic atlas prior with limited fMRI observations yield more accurate cortical visual maps than either source alone?
key_assumptions: an atlas learned across subjects captures a useful prior over retinotopic organization
subject fMRI measurements act as a noisy observation of the true map
areal boundaries and discontinuities are inferable jointly with the map
manipulations: amount of subject-specific fMRI data fed into the model
atlas-only vs measurement-only vs combined Bayesian estimate
key_findings: method automatically draws areal boundaries from combined evidence
discontinuities in measured maps are corrected by the prior
Bayesian estimate predicts validation data better than atlas alone or data alone
interpretations: Bayesian fusion of atlas prior and short fMRI runs improves retinotopic mapping accuracy
approach enables automatic large-scale analysis and quantification of inter-individual map variation
